content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN distinct elements satisfying 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§N1 \le A_i \le N1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§N.

Consider performing the following operation as many times as you want on AAA, while ‚ą£A‚ą£>1|A| > 1‚ą£A‚ą£>1:


Choose a (continuous) subarray‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† of size ‚ą£A‚ą£‚ąí1|A| - 1‚ą£A‚ą£‚ąí1, and then delete the maximum element of the subarray from the original array.

Note that the maximum element is unique due to elements being distinct, and that this decreases the size of AAA by 111 each operation.
Find the number of arrays that can be formed from the above process modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† A subarray is an array that can be formed by deleting some (possibly 000) elements from the front of the array, and some (possibly 000) from the back.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of arrays that can be formed with the operations modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
